entities.name: PLANT POPULATION DENSITY
entities.description: The number of plants per unit area in a field, which can vary from recommended levels.

entities.name: GROWTH DURATION
entities.description: The length of time a plant takes to complete its growth cycle, affecting its ability to compensate for low population density.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural output produced, which may be reduced by low plant population density.

entities.name: PLASTICITY
entities.description: The ability of a cultivar to produce stable yields over a range of population densities.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of selecting for specific traits to improve crop performance and stabilize yields.

entities.name: CULTIVAR
entities.description: A cultivated variety of a plant that exhibits varying degrees of plasticity.

relationships.description: Low plant population density can lead to a reduction in yield.
Longer growth duration can partially overcome the negative effects of low plant population density.
Plasticity enables the production of stable yields across different population densities.
Plasticity is the ability to compensate for yield reduction at lower plant population densities.
A cultivar's ability to maintain stable yields is defined as its plasticity.
Breeding is used to select for plasticity to stabilize yields.
Breeding for plasticity is intended to stabilize yields at the farmer level.
